title: Once Upon A Time In Springfield

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening with Homer's signature exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase itself
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - zero originality at this point but perfect character integration and economy

jokes.text: Excellent.
jokes.punchline: His characteristic understated approval
jokes.explanation: Another signature catchphrase - reliable but not innovative

jokes.text: Don't worry. I'll put out the flames. / Oops! I used the wrong end.
jokes.setup: Mel offers to help with fire extinguisher
jokes.punchline: He used it backwards, making things worse
jokes.explanation: Classic slapstick with good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: Hilarious! / I don't like this. Real humor comes from people being nice to each other.
jokes.punchline: Marge's gentle correction about 'real humor'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Marge's earnestness vs Krusty's crude humor sensibilities

jokes.text: There's nothing funnier than a good old bonk on the head. / I concur. D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets bonked and says 'D'oh!'
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy timing - Homer proves his point by becoming victim of it

jokes.text: please hand back all the hats, toys, and savings bonds I passed out during the taping
jokes.setup: Asking kids to return promotional items
jokes.punchline: Including actual savings bonds among the trinkets
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from cheap toys to valuable financial instruments

jokes.text: if my writers knew how to appeal to girls, they wouldn't be writers
jokes.setup: Network wants him to appeal to girls
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating burn on the writing profession
jokes.explanation: Sharp industry satire with great economy - the logic is both cruel and believable

jokes.text: Then we've got an entire reality show all set to pick the new you
jokes.setup: Krusty threatens to refuse network demands
jokes.punchline: They're already prepared to replace him with reality TV
jokes.explanation: Prescient satire of reality TV's threat to traditional entertainment

jokes.text: Hey, hey! I'm non-union!
jokes.punchline: Offers to work without union protection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's desperation and lack of principles

jokes.text: Make it exactly like the other crap on your network, but just let me stay!
jokes.punchline: Admits he'll make garbage TV to survive
jokes.explanation: Self-aware about TV quality while showing character's desperation

jokes.text: Some rainbow-- there's no violet.
jokes.punchline: CBG's pedantic observation about incomplete spectrum
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - pedantic complaint about trivial detail

jokes.text: Hey, don't listen to me! What do I know about rainbows?
jokes.setup: Just made an authoritative statement about rainbows
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines his own expertise
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing rare self-doubt from CBG

jokes.text: jellies rubbing elbows with cream-filled, the masculine contours of the box juxtaposed with the feminine curves of the treats themselves
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic, sexual description of pastries
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd poetry from Homer - turning donuts into erotic art criticism

jokes.text: Why didn't you try to stop him? / I did, once.
jokes.setup: Question about not stopping Homer's donut soliloquy
jokes.punchline: Marge's weary admission of past failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - resigned acceptance of Homer's quirks

jokes.text: Until Mr. Roosevelt's New Deal starts working, this country's still in a depression
jokes.punchline: References 1930s as if it's current
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns anachronism - his age/disconnect from modern times

jokes.text: I'm spending a fortune on atoms
jokes.punchline: Treats atomic particles as expensive purchase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating fundamental particles like commodities

jokes.text: Yeah, like the ceiling furniture.
jokes.setup: Suggesting more expensive unnecessaries than donuts
jokes.punchline: Furniture attached to the ceiling
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd visual concept - furniture defying gravity for no reason

jokes.text: Or all the joke ID badges we order.
jokes.punchline: Spending money on fake employee IDs for comedy
jokes.explanation: Good absurd bureaucracy joke - ordering fake badges for gags

jokes.text: No! D'oh! Nuts!
jokes.setup: Homer's reaction to no more donuts
jokes.punchline: Combines 'D'oh!', 'No!', and 'nuts' into one exclamation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Homer's catchphrase with the donut context

jokes.text: The network 'geniuses' with the ink still drying on their MBAs
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description of young executives
jokes.explanation: Sharp industry satire about inexperienced executives making creative decisions

jokes.text: I can't keep watching this dreck.
jokes.punchline: Bart's disgusted exit using sophisticated vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart using 'dreck' shows his cultural sophistication

jokes.text: First, girls ruined Sex and the City. Now this.
jokes.punchline: Blaming girls for ruining a show about women
jokes.explanation: Perfect CBG logic - blaming the target audience for a show designed for them

jokes.text: You can see the strap on the horn.
jokes.punchline: Lisa notices the obvious fake horn attachment
jokes.explanation: Lisa's analytical nature deflating the magic with practical observation

jokes.text: Easy there, Habitrail.
jokes.punchline: Moe compares them to a hamster habitat
jokes.explanation: Great Moe insult - creative and character-appropriate putdown

jokes.text: I think that's the best thing 'cause then you can say, 'Well, there's nothing we can do about it.'
jokes.punchline: Barney finds comfort in having an excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney logic - finding the silver lining in hopelessness

jokes.text: You want to 'buy a round?' I heard about that in bartending school, but I never seen it happen.
jokes.setup: Gator offers to buy drinks for everyone
jokes.punchline: Moe treats generosity as a theoretical concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Moe's dive bar so cheap that buying rounds is mythical

jokes.text: I guess these days, headhunters can be anything.
jokes.setup: Gator introduces himself as a headhunter
jokes.punchline: Homer assumes he means literal head-hunting
jokes.explanation: Good Homer misunderstanding of business terminology

jokes.text: now that all the protesters who marched in front of nuclear power plants are dying off from radiation poisoning
jokes.setup: Explaining why nuclear workers are valuable
jokes.punchline: Darkly ironic reason - protesters getting cancer
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic and satirically sharp about nuclear industry consequences

jokes.text: Are these business cards or passports to a better future? / Those are business cards.
jokes.setup: Homer's poetic interpretation of business cards
jokes.punchline: Gator's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's grandiose metaphor with literal response

jokes.text: Girls don't laugh, and they don't buy cigars.
jokes.punchline: Krusty's practical concern about revenue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty logic - reducing audience value to cigar sales

jokes.text: Breathin' all this stuff can't be good.
jokes.setup: Princess character using lots of glitter
jokes.punchline: Bart's practical health concern
jokes.explanation: Bart's practical response to magical excess

jokes.text: He's become the lowest form of life: a sidekick.
jokes.punchline: Treating sidekick as biological classification
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor treating entertainment hierarchy as evolution

jokes.text: Do you want to come in and get your sister with me? / Hell, no. / Watch your language. Now, why the hell not?
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses same profanity herself
jokes.explanation: Classic parental hypocrisy joke - Marge's double standard

jokes.text: Why are great things always ruined by women? The Army, the Fantastic Four.
jokes.punchline: Lumping military with comic books
jokes.explanation: Absurd equivalence between serious institution and comic franchise

jokes.text: Think how awesome American Idol would be with just Simon and Randy.
jokes.punchline: Wanting to remove Paula Abdul from Idol
jokes.explanation: Topical reference but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, Bart, you say that now. But when you're grown up, you'll just think it.
jokes.punchline: Marge's cynical wisdom about adult discretion
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark wisdom from Marge about how prejudice becomes internalized

jokes.text: One of Look magazine's hundred most-promising clowns of 1958. / A lot of suicides in that group.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to cheer Krusty with past accolade
jokes.punchline: Krusty's dark revelation about fellow honorees
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - turning career achievement into death statistic

jokes.text: Funny suicides, but still.
jokes.punchline: Qualifier that they were at least amusing deaths
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Krusty logic - even suicide should be entertaining. Peak character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, why do clown things always happen to clowns?
jokes.punchline: Tautological complaint about professional hazards
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - circular reasoning about occupational hazards

jokes.text: Man, a lion would not want to see that on his X-ray.
jokes.setup: Gary Larson drawing cartoon for nuclear workers
jokes.punchline: Classic Far Side style anthropomorphic animal humor
jokes.explanation: Good pastiche of Gary Larson's actual style and subject matter

jokes.text: I wasn't popular because I was more beautiful and friendly than everyone else
jokes.punchline: Blaming positive qualities for social rejection
jokes.explanation: Good character delusionabout why she was unpopular

jokes.text: Welcome to the Krusty and Princess Penelope Smoochie-Poo I Love You Hour. No laughs, just hugs and cuddles.
jokes.punchline: Explicitly anti-comedy children's show
jokes.explanation: Absurd concept - children's show that actively avoids humor

jokes.text: 'Cause I got a girlfriend under 33...
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing girlfriend's age inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable age-gap humor characteristic of Krusty's inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: I walked 19 miles for this?
jokes.punchline: Audience member's buyer's remorse for long journey
jokes.explanation: Standard audience disappointment reaction with specific distance detail

jokes.text: I don't care what anyone says. Massages are relaxing.
jokes.setup: Homer getting massage at competing plant
jokes.punchline: Stating obvious benefit as controversial opinion
jokes.explanation: Homer treating universally accepted fact as brave stance

jokes.text: Comparing me to old Draco, eh?
jokes.punchline: Burns takes it as personal comparison to ancient lawgiver
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns moment - treating historical reference as personal acquaintance

jokes.text: Milhouse Van Houten, will you do me the honor of ruining Krusty's wedding with me? / Oh, Bart, this is all happening so fast. Let's call my mom together.
jokes.punchline: Milhouse treats it like romantic proposal
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Milhouse's nervous reaction mirrors romantic comedy

jokes.text: These doughnuts were made the old-fashioned way: the dough is sweetened with Cuban sugar from pre-Batista plantations, then it's deep-fried in the tallow of three different animals, two of which are now extinct.
jokes.setup: Burns offering special donuts to retain workers
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and morally questionable ingredients
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity - from political history to extinct species in donut recipe

jokes.text: One of these every day might kill us. / Can we get a health plan to go with them? / Sure, you can have a health plan... or two doughnuts a day.
jokes.punchline: Burns offering more donuts instead of health insurance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns logic - solving health concerns with more health hazards

jokes.text: Move over, Brangelina. Take a back seat, Queen Latifarod. Because today belongs to 'Krustelope.'
jokes.punchline: Celebrity couple portmanteau with made-up Queen Latifah reference
jokes.explanation: Standard entertainment media parody with forced celebrity mashup

jokes.text: Her actual name is: Penelope Mountbatten Hapsburg Hohenzollern Mulan-Pocahontas.
jokes.setup: Revealing Princess Penelope isn't real royalty
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously long name mixing real and Disney royalty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation mixing European royal houses with Disney characters

jokes.text: to marry a Jew and a... Congregationalist-- is that even a thing?
jokes.punchline: Uncertainty about Congregationalism's existence
jokes.explanation: Good religious humor about obscure Protestant denomination

jokes.text: I locked him in the Torah room.
jokes.setup: Krusty asks what happened to Mr. Teeny
jokes.punchline: Bart imprisoned monkey in religious study room
jokes.explanation: Absurd monkey containment method with religious space

jokes.text: He wouldn't let me watch the moon landing. / I was jealous of Neil Armstrong.
jokes.punchline: Krusty's petty jealousy of astronaut
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty pettiness - jealous of humanity's greatest achievement

jokes.text: They were only married for six hours, but she still hated Krusty. / He was asleep for five of those hours. / And the one he was awake, was a cat-tastrophe.
jokes.punchline: Most time sleeping, plus cat pun on her name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with character-specific pun on Eartha Kitt's name

jokes.text: And that's why I can't marry you. / This kid and his ugly sister are right.
jokes.punchline: Insulting Lisa while agreeing with Bart
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty inappropriateness - gratuitous insult during serious moment

jokes.text: Princess, you're the only woman I care about enough to ditch at the altar.
jokes.punchline: Romantic gesture through abandonment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty logic - romantic gesture through ultimate rejection. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Ah, I'd rather be a happy schnook than a noble shlumpf.
jokes.punchline: Yiddish philosophy about happiness vs. dignity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with authentic Yiddish wisdom - perfect Krusty philosophy

tmdb_overview: Krusty grudgingly adds a pretty, singing cohost named Princess Penelope to his show in order to attract more female viewers. Girls love her, and ratings improve, but Bart and Milhouse want to get the show back to the way it was. The situation gets more complicated when Krusty and Penelope fall in love and decide to get married. Meanwhile, Homer, Lenny, and Carl consider working at a competing nuclear plant when Mr. Burns cuts off their daily doughnut service.
